description_long: This object appears to be a heavily weathered and partially damaged airplane, with a metallic fuselage and visible structural elements. The wings are broad and extend from the upper section of the fuselage, showing signs of corrosion and wear. There are several exposed pipes or cables running along the top and sides, and the surface is marked by patches of rust and discoloration. The landing gear is partially visible, with what looks like two main wheels and some mechanical components exposed. The cockpit area is identifiable by a small window and a door, both showing signs of age and use. The overall appearance suggests an industrial or utilitarian aircraft, possibly used in harsh environments.
description: A weathered, metallic airplane with broad upper wings, exposed piping, and visible signs of corrosion and damage.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the broad, upper wing and exposed piping, with significant surface wear and a partially visible landing gear.
description_view_1: The rear-left view shows the back of the fuselage, more exposed cables, and the weathered tail section.
description_view_2: The right side view reveals the underside of the wing, additional mechanical components, and more of the landing gear structure.
description_view_3: The front-right view displays the cockpit window, door, and the forward section of the fuselage, with visible surface corrosion.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the fuselage or wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main wheels and a nose wheel visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single window and door visible.
